hallucinations.description: Several references cite nonexistent or future-dated papers (e.g., Zheng et al. 2025 arXiv:2511.12863, Bühler et al. 2026 arXiv:2601.04110, ECOVAL 2024, Libra 2024). These cannot be verified and appear fabricated.
hallucinations.evidence: References section: items 4, 6, 9, 10 list arXiv IDs and years (2025/2026) with no matching real publications.

hallucinations.description: The paper describes advanced causal extensions (do-calculus, cross-stage Jacobians, Hessian inverses, low-rank SVD on Jacobians, counterfactual estimation via Pearl's calculus) but the actual code implements only simple gradient dot-products with no stage propagation, Jacobians, or Hessians.
hallucinations.evidence: Paper sections 3.1.2-3.1.3 and 3.2.2-3.2.3 vs. valuation_methods.py (StageAwareInfluence._compute_stage_influence and StandardInfluence are identical) and trainer.py (no Jacobian or Hessian code); paper section 6.3 explicitly admits the implementations are identical.

hallucinations.description: Results and figures are presented as demonstrating clear stage-aware advantages and statistical signals, yet code shows identical performance to the standard baseline, non-significant p-values, and empty/failed visualization outputs for key plots.
hallucinations.evidence: Log.txt aggregated results (0.31 ± 0.20, p≈0.26) and paper Tables 2-3/Figure 3 vs. actual code output showing no differentiation; visualize_results.py plots for correlation/computation time are blank or missing data.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations were found: nonexistent citations, substantial mismatch between claimed advanced causal methodology and the simple/non-differentiated implementation in code, and overstated experimental claims/figures. Severity is moderate-high due to fabricated references and core methodological misrepresentation.
